name: Dehesa Boyal, Embalse de Santillana y Puente de la Marmota — circular por Madrid Norte
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around San Sebastián de los Reyes offer a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region features a combination of urban parks, wetlands, and significant natural areas like the Dehesa Boyal and Monte Coto Pesadilla. Its varied topography includes more pronounced reliefs with moderate slopes in the west, contrasting with flatter areas influenced by the Jarama River. This mix of holm oak forests, riverine paths, and open terrain provides a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around San Sebastián De Los Reyes The most popular mountain bike trail is From the Dehesa Boyal to the Barajas Airport - circular route through Madrid North, a 35.0 miles (56.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects natural areas with urban outskirts, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is San Sebastián de los Reyes and San Agustín del Guadalix Loop, a moderate 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route explores two municipalities, featuring riverine landscapes and open fields. Local mountain bikers also love the Monte de Valdelatas and Dehesa Boyal Loop, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail leading through protected natural enclaves, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around San Sebastián de los Reyes is defined by extensive holm oak forests, river valleys, and varied topography ranging from flat plains to moderate slopes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in San Sebastián de los Reyes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore San Sebastián de los Reyes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: There are over 45 mountain bike trails in San Sebastián de los Reyes, offering a wide range of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from the more pronounced reliefs and moderate slopes in the western part, featuring wooded pasturelands, to flatter areas influenced by the Jarama River. You'll find extensive holm oak forests (encinares), riverine paths along the Jarama and Guadalix rivers, and well-trodden paths suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: Yes, San Sebastián de los Reyes has trails suitable for different ability levels. Out of the 48 available routes, 14 are classified as easy, making them a good option for beginners or family outings. The region's varied topography includes wide and well-trodden paths that are ideal for less experienced riders.

Q: What are some scenic natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in the area?
A: Mountain biking in San Sebastián de los Reyes allows you to explore significant natural areas like the Dehesa Boyal and Monte Coto Pesadilla, which are part of the Regional Park of the Upper Manzanares Basin. You can also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6344971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hervidero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/957559" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdeliganar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5147399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelatas Forest&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often follow the Jarama and Guadalix rivers, offering varied vegetation and rich amphibian and bird fauna.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1333664265/from-the-dehesa-boyal-to-the-barajas-airport-circular-route-through-madrid-north" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From the Dehesa Boyal to the Barajas Airport - circular route through Madrid North&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 56.4 km trail. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1333675149/monte-de-valdelatas-and-dehesa-boyal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Valdelatas and Dehesa Boyal Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 33.7 km long and takes you through protected natural enclaves.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: The mountain bike trails in San Sebastián de los Reyes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, which combines natural areas like the Dehesa Boyal with riverine paths and varied topography, offering engaging experiences for all levels.

Q: Are there more challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking more challenge, there are 10 difficult routes available. These trails often feature the more pronounced reliefs and moderate slopes found in the western part of the municipality. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1333716682/dehesa-boyal-y-trialeras-de-el-pardo-circular-por-madrid-norte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesa Boyal y Trialeras de El Pardo — circular por Madrid Norte&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 53.3 km route with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find trails that connect different municipalities or natural parks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in San Sebastián de los Reyes are designed to explore broader areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963992034/san-sebastian-de-los-reyes-and-san-agustin-del-guadalix-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Sebastián de los Reyes and San Agustín del Guadalix Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 60.2 km path that explores two municipalities, featuring riverine landscapes and open fields. Other routes traverse protected areas like the Regional Park of the Upper Manzanares Basin.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in San Sebastián de los Reyes?
A: San Sebastián de los Reyes offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even in winter, the region's varied terrain, including holm oak forests and well-trodden paths, can be enjoyable, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like lakes or wetlands?
A: Yes, the municipality is home to a hidden wetland formed by the Viñuelas stream before it flows into the Jarama river, which can be explored. Additionally, some routes may pass by natural water features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2859350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Cobeña&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7019544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Clean Pond&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic views and opportunities to observe local wildlife.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails in San Sebastián de los Reyes well-marked?
A: The region is known for its 'caminos anchos y bien pisados' (wide and well-trodden paths), which generally makes navigation straightforward. Komoot routes provide detailed maps and navigation to help you stay on track, ensuring a smooth riding experience.

A very nice reservoir. The sunsets and sunrises are spectacular, there are incredible views of Manzanares and its castle, and it is a good place to photograph birds.

This is the best preserved castle in the community of Madrid. It is also known as the Mendoza castle because it was this family that built it to reinforce their dominance and influence in 15th-century Castile. Juan Guas, who was the architect of the Catholic Monarchs, participated in its construction.

Its wall testifies to its function as a fortress while its large interior rooms tell of the arrival of new times with less warriors and more palatial and social life. It currently has an interpretation center of the Middle Ages and it carries out outreach activities on this important period in Spain and other cultural activities.
The route between the reservoir and the Batán bridge is recommended

Enjoy the views, the mountains and the Castle of Manzanares in the background, the Santillana reservoir at your feet.

Section of the GR-124, also known as Senda Real, is a long-distance trail that runs through the Community of Madrid (Spain). It begins at the Príncipe Pío Station and ends, after a route of 47.5 kilometers, in the town of Manzanares el Real, in the Guadarrama mountain range. Although it was approved as GR in 1999, its origin dates back to the old path that linked the Real Alcázar of Madrid with the El Pardo palace, although the current route also uses other historic paths and livestock trails.

Dates from the 18th century. It was ordered to be built, directly, by Ferdinand VI to give continuity to the surveillance of the fence of the Mount of El Pardo, a fence that he also had built to prevent poaching within the mount, a royal hunting reserve.
